questions.text: What is the primary purpose of client-side programming technologies?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To respond to requests for searching the Internet

choices.label: B
choices.text: To build applications that run in the user's browser

choices.label: C
choices.text: To manage bank-account balances on a remote server

choices.label: D
choices.text: To store structured sets of data in a database

questions.explanation: Client-side technologies are used to build web pages and applications that run on the client, specifically in the browser on the user’s device.

questions.text: Which organizations collaborated to create the HTML5 version of the language?
questions.explanation: HTML5 is a cooperation between the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C) and the Web Hypertext Application Technology Working Group (WHATWG).

questions.text: Which of the following is a key rule established for HTML5 development?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Increase reliance on external plugins like Flash

choices.label: B
choices.text: Reduce the need for external plugins

choices.label: C
choices.text: Keep the development process private from the public

choices.label: D
choices.text: New features should be based on XML only

questions.explanation: One of the fundamental rules of HTML5 is to reduce the need for external plugins like Flash.

questions.text: What does the term 'portable' mean in the context of Internet and web programming?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The code can only be used on smartphones

choices.label: B
choices.text: The language is easy to translate to other languages

choices.label: C
choices.text: Applications can run across an enormous range of Internet-enabled devices

choices.label: D
choices.text: The website can be easily downloaded to a USB drive

questions.explanation: Portability allows designing web pages and applications that run across a wide range of Internet-enabled devices, from smartphones to large-screen displays.

questions.text: Which HTML5 element is specifically designed for 2D drawing?
questions.explanation: The <canvas> element is one of the new features in HTML5 used for 2D drawing.

questions.text: Which of the following are new content-specific semantic elements in HTML5?
questions.explanation: HTML5 introduced new content-specific elements like <article>, <footer>, <header>, <nav>, and <section>.

questions.text: The document type declaration (\( <!DOCTYPE html> \)) is required in HTML5 to:

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ensure the browser renders the page in quirks mode

choices.label: B
choices.text: Specify the version of CSS being used

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ensure the browser renders the page in standards mode

choices.label: D
choices.text: Link the document to a web server

questions.explanation: The DOCTYPE is required so that browsers render the page in standards mode rather than quirks mode.

questions.text: In HTML5, comments are delimited by which of the following?
questions.explanation: HTML5 comments start with <!-- and end with -->.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the \( \text{head} \) section in an HTML5 document?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To contain the actual content visible to the user

choices.label: B
choices.text: To contain information about the document, such as title and character set

choices.label: C
choices.text: To define the main footer of the webpage

choices.label: D
choices.text: To store images and videos directly

questions.explanation: The head section contains information about the document, including the title, character set (UTF-8), and formatting instructions.

questions.text: What is UTF-8?

choices.label: C
choices.text: A variable-width character encoding for Unicode

questions.explanation: UTF-8 is a variable-width encoding that can represent every character in the Unicode character set.

questions.text: Elements that do not have an end tag, such as \( \text{} \), are called:
questions.explanation: Elements like that contain only attributes and no text content between tags are known as void elements.

questions.text: Where does the text within the \( \text{<title>} \) element typically appear?

choices.label: A
choices.text: In the main body of the webpage

choices.label: B
choices.text: In the browser's title bar or tab

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the very bottom of the document

choices.label: D
choices.text: As the largest heading on the page

questions.explanation: Titles usually appear in the title bar at the top of the browser window or in the browser tab.

questions.text: Which heading element represents the most significant heading?
questions.explanation: <h1> is the most significant heading and is rendered in the largest font by default.

questions.text: What does the \( \text{href} \) attribute in an \( \text{<a>} \) tag specify?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The font style of the link

choices.label: D
choices.text: The name of the user clicking the link

questions.explanation: The href (hypertext reference) attribute specifies the location of the resource, such as a web page, file, or email address.

questions.text: Which protocol is used in an anchor tag to link to an e-mail address?
questions.explanation: Anchors link to email addresses using the mailto: URL scheme.

questions.text: Why is the \( \text{alt} \) attribute mandatory for the \( \text{} \) element?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It specifies the alternative color of the image

choices.label: B
choices.text: It improves accessibility for visually impaired users using speech synthesizers

choices.label: C
choices.text: It links the image to a high-resolution version

choices.label: D
choices.text: It is required for the image to load faster

questions.explanation: The alt attribute provides a text description of the image, which is essential for accessibility and displayed if the image fails to render.

questions.text: What is the character entity reference for the ampersand (\( \text{&} \)) symbol?
questions.explanation: The ampersand symbol is represented by the character entity reference &.

questions.text: Which element is used to create an unordered list with bullet points?
questions.explanation: The <ul> (unordered list) element creates a list where each item begins with a bullet symbol.

questions.text: How do you specify the title of an HTML5 table?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Using the <title> tag inside the table

questions.explanation: The <caption> element is used to specify a title for a table.

questions.text: Which attribute allows a table cell to span across multiple columns?
questions.explanation: The colspan attribute specifies the number of columns a cell should occupy.

questions.text: In a form, which \( \text{method} \) appends form data to the URL, making it visible in the address bar?
questions.explanation: The 'get' method appends form data directly to the end of the URL, making it visible to the user.

questions.text: What is the function of the \( \text{<label>} \) element in a form?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To submit the form to the server

choices.label: B
choices.text: To provide a multiline text area

choices.label: C
choices.text: To provide users with information about an input element's purpose

choices.label: D
choices.text: To group multiple radio buttons together

questions.explanation: The <label> element is used to provide a descriptive label for form inputs, improving usability and accessibility.

questions.text: Which input type allows a user to select only one option from a group of mutually exclusive choices?
questions.explanation: Radio buttons are used when only one option in a group can be selected at a time.

questions.text: How do you link to a specific section of the same document (internal linking)?

choices.label: A
choices.text: By using the href='#id' syntax where 'id' matches the target element's id

choices.label: B
choices.text: By using the <link> tag with the name of the section

choices.label: C
choices.text: By creating a new .html file for every section

choices.label: D
choices.text: Internal linking is not possible in HTML5

questions.explanation: Internal linking is achieved by setting an 'id' on the target element and using '#id' as the href value in an anchor tag.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the \( \text{meta} \) element with the \( \text{name='description'} \) attribute?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To define the character encoding of the page

choices.label: B
choices.text: To provide a list of keywords for search engines

choices.label: C
choices.text: To provide a three- to four-line summary used by search engines to catalog the site

choices.label: D
choices.text: To specify the author of the document

questions.explanation: The description meta element provides a short summary of the site, which search engines often display in search results.

questions.text: Which of the following is considered a 'legacy' element that should generally be avoided in favor of CSS?
questions.explanation:
According to the text, the
(horizontal rule) and
(line break) elements are considered legacy formatting elements that should be replaced by CSS.

questions.text: Which element is used to insert a multiline text field in a form?
questions.explanation: The <textarea> element is used for multiline user input.

questions.text: In a table, which element contains the primary data of the table?
questions.explanation: The <tbody> element contains the primary table data.

questions.text: What does the \( \text{strong} \) element signify?

choices.label: A
choices.text: That the text is a link

choices.label: B
choices.text: That the text has high importance and should typically be bold

choices.label: C
choices.text: That the text is a computer code

choices.label: D
choices.text: That the text is a list item

questions.explanation: The <strong> element indicates high importance, and browsers usually render it in bold.

questions.text: Which attribute of the \( \text{table} \) element is used to make it more accessible by describing its content to speech devices?
questions.explanation: The summary attribute provides a description of the table's purpose for assistive technologies.

questions.text: Which element is used to define an individual row in a table?
questions.explanation: The <tr> (table row) element defines a row in an HTML table.

questions.text: What happens if a web server cannot find a requested HTML5 document?
questions.explanation: If a server cannot locate a resource, it returns a 404 error.

questions.text: To create a button that clears all user input in a form, which \( \text{type} \) attribute should be used for the \( \text{<input>} \) element?
questions.explanation: The <input type='reset'> element creates a button that resets all form fields to their default values.

questions.text: What is the role of a web server?

choices.label: B
choices.text: To store HTML5 documents and respond to client requests

choices.label: C
choices.text: To create images for web pages

choices.label: D
choices.text: To physically connect cables to the Internet

questions.explanation: Web servers are computers that store documents and provide them to clients (browsers) upon request.

questions.text: Which of these is NOT a browser mentioned in the text as having support for HTML5 features?
questions.explanation: WordPad is a text editor, not a web browser. The text mentions Safari, Chrome, Firefox, Opera, and Internet Explorer.

questions.text: What is the significance of 'nesting' in HTML?

choices.label: A
choices.text: It refers to placing one element inside another

choices.label: B
choices.text: It refers to the speed of the website

choices.label: C
choices.text: It refers to deleting old code

choices.label: D
choices.text: It refers to connecting two different websites

questions.explanation: Nesting occurs when elements are enclosed within other elements, such as the <title> being nested within the <head>.

questions.text: Which element provides a drop-down list of items in a form?
questions.explanation: The <select> element, combined with <option> elements, creates a drop-down menu.

questions.text: What does the \( \text{action} \) attribute of a \( \text{form} \) element define?

choices.label: B
choices.text: The script or URL to which the form data will be sent

choices.label: C
choices.text: The background color of the form

choices.label: D
choices.text: The type of buttons used in the form

questions.explanation: The action attribute specifies the destination (form handler) for the data when the form is submitted.

questions.text: Which of the following is a new form control introduced in HTML5?
questions.explanation: HTML5 introduced new form controls like calendar, date, time, email, and url.

questions.text: When using the \( \text{post} \) method in a form, where is the data placed?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the end of the URL

choices.label: C
choices.text: Appended to the browser request body

questions.explanation: The 'post' method appends form data to the browser request body rather than the URL.

questions.text: What is the purpose of 'hidden' inputs in forms?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To hide the entire form from the user

choices.label: B
choices.text: To store data that should not be visible to the user but sent to the server

choices.label: C
choices.text: To encrypt the data being sent

choices.label: D
choices.text: To prevent the user from clicking the submit button

questions.explanation: Hidden inputs are nonvisual components used to store data, such as email addresses or file names, that need to be processed by the server.

questions.text: Which tag is used for the largest heading and which for the smallest heading in HTML5?
questions.explanation: HTML5 provides six heading elements, <h1> through <h6>, where <h1> is the largest.

questions.text: The \( \text{<hr>} \) tag is used to render a:
questions.explanation: The <hr> tag renders a horizontal line used to separate content.

questions.text: What is the function of the W3C Validation Service?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To host your website for free

choices.label: B
choices.text: To ensure an HTML5 document is syntactically correct

choices.label: D
choices.text: To check the speed of the Internet connection

questions.explanation: Validation services like W3C's ensure that HTML documents follow the correct syntax rules, which helps guarantee proper rendering.

questions.text: Which image formats are mentioned as the most popular for web development?
questions.explanation: The text identifies PNG (Portable Network Graphics) and JPEG (Joint Photographic Experts Group) as the most popular formats.

questions.text: In a table, the \( \text{<tfoot>} \) section appears where in the page display?

choices.label: A
choices.text: At the top, above the header

choices.label: B
choices.text: In the middle of the body

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the bottom of the table

choices.label: D
choices.text: It does not display on the page

questions.explanation: Although coded above the <tbody> in some versions for processing reasons, it displays at the bottom of the table.

questions.text: Which attribute limits the number of characters a user can enter into a text field?
questions.explanation: The optional maxlength attribute restricts how many characters can be typed into an input field.

questions.text: To mask sensitive information like passwords, which input \( \text{type} \) should be used?
questions.explanation: The 'password' input type masks characters (usually with asterisks) to protect sensitive data from being seen.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the \( \text{<thead>} \) element in a table?

choices.label: A
choices.text: To store the main data rows

choices.label: B
choices.text: To define the table's header section, including column titles

choices.label: C
choices.text: To link the table to a database

choices.label: D
choices.text: To provide a caption for the table

questions.explanation: The <thead> element defines the header section of a table, typically containing column headers.

questions.text: What does the abbreviation XHTML stand for?
questions.explanation: XHTML stands for EXtensible HyperText Markup Language.
